title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Life-Long Income vs Long-Term Income: What Should You Opt For?
One thought that strikes people while planning their future is how to secure a steady income after retirement. Two popular options are life long income or long term income. These come with guaranteed income plans or savings plans. They provide a regular source of income, either for a few years or for the rest of your life, depending on the plan you choose. This steady source of income can help you pay bills, handle medical costs, or manage your day-to-day needs. But which option should you choose? This depends on your age, goals, and how long you want to receive the income.
Understanding the Life Long Income Plan
A life long income plan is an insurance plan that provides regular income for your whole life. Life long income plans keep paying you money every year for life.
It helps the life assured get steady money after retirement or during old age.
The plan uses concepts such as the present value of an annuity to calculate how much income you will get.
It combines insurance and investment planning to provide financial support and protection.
What is Long Term Income Plan?
A long term income plan pays an income over a fixed time frame. These payouts begin after a specified time period, known as the "policy term." It doesn't continue till life, but if you have a specific financial need over a set number of years, this can help in your planning.
This option is suitable for those who want to save for a specific goal, such as their children’s higher education, paying off loans, or managing household expenses during early retirement.
You buy the plan and pay for a few years.
After the plan starts, you get regular payouts for a fixed time only.
These payouts help handle regular needs for a certain number of years.
Such a plan is also a part of insurance and investment planning and may offer lump sum and income benefits depending on the plan you choose.
Difference Between Life Long Income and Long Term Income Plans
Let us now understand the life long income or long term income difference clearly. These two plans may seem similar, but they are meant for different needs.
The life long or long term income plan you choose should depend on your personal financial goal, your age, and how long you want to receive regular income.
If you want to stay financially stable for the rest of your life after retirement, a life long income plan is suitable.
If you need support for a shorter term and a fixed number of years, like during your early retirement, a long-term plan is better.
Both plans play a role in your insurance and investment planning journey. When selecting a plan, always consider your needs and the payout period, which indicates how much money today will be worth in the future.
Conclusion
Selecting between life long income or long term income plan is based on your financial objectives. If you require money throughout your entire life, a lifelong income plan can take care of you. But if you just require steady income for some years, a long term plan is a suitable option. Both the options have significant implications in insurance and investment planning, enabling policyholders to strike a balance between steady income and flexibility through the years.
FAQs
1. Which policy gives a monthly income?
Policies that offer monthly income are known as annuity plans. These include life long income plans and long term income plans. Once you pay into the plan for a specified period, the policy begins providing a regular income. These help maintain regular cash flow after retirement or during special life phases.
2. Are long term plans a good option?
The long-term income plan could be a good option if you want to have income for a fixed number of years. They help you manage planned goals such as education, marriage expenses, or EMIs. Long-term income plans offer fixed payouts, which allow us to plan our fixed monthly expenses much more reliably.
